content.text: User-Assigned Managed Identity

A user-assigned managed identity is a standalone Azure resource that you create independently of any compute resource. You then attach (associate) it to one or more Azure resources. Unlike system-assigned identities — where each resource gets its own dedicated service principal — a single user-assigned identity can be shared across many resources. This makes it ideal for fleets of VMs, autoscaling scenarios, and any architecture where multiple services need the same set of permissions.

attrs.alt: User-assigned managed identity shared across VM-1, VM-2, App Service, and Function App — one set of RBAC roles used by all

content.text: System-Assigned vs User-Assigned — Key Differences
Property
System-Assigned
User-Assigned
Lifecycle
Tied to the resource — deleted with it
Independent — survives resource deletion
Sharing
One identity per resource
One identity shared across many resources
Pre-provisioning
Created at resource creation time
Can be created before any compute resource exists
Principal ID stability
Changes if the resource is deleted and recreated
Stable — same principal ID always
RBAC role assignments
Assigned per-resource
Assigned once; all attached resources inherit them
Use case
Single-resource, simple scenarios
Shared permissions, autoscaling, pre-provisioning
Creating a User-Assigned Identity
Azure CLI

# Create the user-assigned identity as a standalone resource
az identity create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --location eastus

# The output includes:
# - id: the ARM resource ID of the identity
# - clientId: used by DefaultAzureCredential to pick this identity
# - principalId: Entra ID object ID — used for role assignments
az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --query '{clientId:clientId, principalId:principalId}' \
    --output table
Azure PowerShell

# Create the identity
$identity = New-AzUserAssignedIdentity `
    -ResourceGroupName 'MyResourceGroup' `
    -Name 'my-shared-identity' `
    -Location 'eastus'

Write-Host 'Client ID:    ' $identity.ClientId
Write-Host 'Principal ID: ' $identity.PrincipalId
Write-Host 'Resource ID:  ' $identity.Id
Bicep
Assigning RBAC Roles — Once for All Resources

You assign roles to the user-assigned identity's principal ID once. Every resource that attaches the identity automatically inherits its permissions. This is the key operational advantage: onboarding a new VM to a fleet only requires attaching the identity, not creating a new role assignment.

# Get the principal ID of the user-assigned identity
PRINCIPAL_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --query principalId --output tsv)

# Get the Key Vault resource ID
KV_ID=$(az keyvault show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyKeyVault \
    --query id --output tsv)

# Assign Key Vault Secrets User — applies to ALL resources that attach this identity
az role assignment create \
    --assignee-object-id "$PRINCIPAL_ID" \
    --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal \
    --role 'Key Vault Secrets User' \
    --scope "$KV_ID"
Attaching the Identity to Resources
CLI — Attach to VM

# Get the full resource ID of the user-assigned identity
IDENTITY_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --query id --output tsv)

# Attach to a VM
az vm identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyVM \
    --identities "$IDENTITY_ID"

# Attach to an App Service
az webapp identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyWebApp \
    --identities "$IDENTITY_ID"

# Attach to a Function App
az functionapp identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyFunctionApp \
    --identities "$IDENTITY_ID"
Bicep — Attach to VM
Terraform — Create, Assign Role, and Attach
Specifying the Identity in Application Code

When a resource has only one identity attached, DefaultAzureCredential selects it automatically. When multiple identities are attached (a resource can have both system-assigned and multiple user-assigned), you must specify which identity to use by passing its client ID.
Python

import os
from azure.identity import ManagedIdentityCredential, DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.keyvault.secrets import SecretClient

# Option A: Explicitly specify the user-assigned identity by client ID
# Required when multiple identities are attached to the resource
client_id  = os.environ['AZURE_CLIENT_ID']   # set this in app settings
credential = ManagedIdentityCredential(client_id=client_id)

# Option B: DefaultAzureCredential with client_id hint
# credential = DefaultAzureCredential(managed_identity_client_id=client_id)

client = SecretClient('https://mykeyvault.vault.azure.net', credential=credential)
secret = client.get_secret('db-connection-string')
print(secret.value)
Environment Variable Pattern

# Set AZURE_CLIENT_ID in the resource's environment/app settings
# DefaultAzureCredential reads this automatically to pick the right identity
az webapp config appsettings set \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyWebApp \
    --settings AZURE_CLIENT_ID='<client-id-of-user-assigned-identity>'

# Your Python/Node/Java/.NET code then uses DefaultAzureCredential()
# without any code change — it reads AZURE_CLIENT_ID from the environment
Using Both System-Assigned and User-Assigned on the Same Resource

A single Azure resource can have both a system-assigned identity and one or more user-assigned identities attached simultaneously. This is useful when the resource needs its own unique identity for some operations AND a shared identity for others.
Lifecycle Operations
Detach an Identity from a Resource

# Detach a user-assigned identity from a VM (identity resource survives)
IDENTITY_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --query id --output tsv)

az vm identity remove \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyVM \
    --identities "$IDENTITY_ID"

# The identity resource and its role assignments still exist
# Only the association with this VM is removed
Delete the Identity Resource

# Permanently delete the user-assigned identity
# This automatically detaches it from all resources and removes role assignments
az identity delete \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity

# Verify deletion
az identity list \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --query '[].{Name:name, ClientId:clientId}' \
    --output table
KQL: Auditing User-Assigned Identity Usage

// Find all sign-ins by a specific user-assigned managed identity
// Replace the display name with the identity's name in Entra ID
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ServicePrincipalName == 'my-shared-identity'
| summarize
    Calls      = count(),
    Resources  = make_set(ResourceDisplayName),
    LastCall   = max(TimeGenerated)
    by ServicePrincipalName, IPAddress
| order by Calls desc
User-Assigned Identity with Azure Container Instances

Azure Container Instances (ACI) frequently uses user-assigned identities because containers are ephemeral — they may be stopped, restarted, or scaled out at any time. A user-assigned identity provides a stable credential that survives container restarts and can be shared across a container group.

# Deploy a container group with a user-assigned identity
IDENTITY_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --query id --output tsv)

az container create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-container \
    --image myacr.azurecr.io/myapp:latest \
    --assign-identity "$IDENTITY_ID" \
    --environment-variables AZURE_CLIENT_ID=$(az identity show \
        --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
        --name my-shared-identity \
        --query clientId --output tsv) \
    --registry-login-server myacr.azurecr.io \
    --cpu 1 --memory 1.5

# The container's code uses DefaultAzureCredential or ManagedIdentityCredential(client_id=...)
User-Assigned Identity with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)

In AKS, user-assigned managed identities are used at two levels: the cluster control plane identity (for managing Azure resources like load balancers and disks) and workload identities for pods. The AKS Workload Identity add-on federates a Kubernetes service account with a user-assigned managed identity, allowing pod-level identity without node-level credential sharing.
Assign a User-Assigned Identity to an AKS Cluster

# Create the user-assigned identity for the AKS cluster control plane
az identity create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name aks-control-plane-identity

IDENTITY_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name aks-control-plane-identity \
    --query id --output tsv)

# Create AKS cluster using the user-assigned identity
az aks create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyAKSCluster \
    --enable-managed-identity \
    --assign-identity "$IDENTITY_ID" \
    --enable-workload-identity \
    --enable-oidc-issuer \
    --node-count 3 \
    --generate-ssh-keys
Workload Identity for a Pod (Service Account Federation)

# Create a user-assigned identity for the workload (pod-level)
az identity create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-workload-identity

WORKLOAD_CLIENT_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-workload-identity \
    --query clientId --output tsv)

WORKLOAD_PRINCIPAL=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-workload-identity \
    --query principalId --output tsv)

# Get the AKS OIDC issuer URL
OIDC_ISSUER=$(az aks show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyAKSCluster \
    --query oidcIssuerProfile.issuerUrl --output tsv)

# Create a federated credential: trust tokens from the AKS OIDC issuer
# for service account 'my-service-account' in namespace 'default'
az identity federated-credential create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --identity-name my-workload-identity \
    --name aks-federation \
    --issuer "$OIDC_ISSUER" \
    --subject 'system:serviceaccount:default:my-service-account' \
    --audience api://AzureADTokenExchange

# Grant the workload identity Key Vault access
KV_ID=$(az keyvault show --name MyKeyVault --query id --output tsv)
az role assignment create \
    --assignee-object-id "$WORKLOAD_PRINCIPAL" \
    --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal \
    --role 'Key Vault Secrets User' \
    --scope "$KV_ID"
PowerShell — Audit All Identities and Attachments

Connect-AzAccount

# List all user-assigned identities in a subscription
Get-AzUserAssignedIdentity | Format-Table Name, ResourceGroupName, ClientId, PrincipalId

# Find all resources in a subscription that have a specific identity attached
$identityName = 'my-shared-identity'
$identityId   = (Get-AzUserAssignedIdentity -Name $identityName -ResourceGroupName 'MyResourceGroup').Id

# Search VMs with this identity
Get-AzVM | Where-Object {
    $_.Identity.UserAssignedIdentities.Keys -contains $identityId
} | Format-Table Name, ResourceGroupName

# Search App Service plans
Get-AzWebApp | Where-Object {
    $_.Identity.UserAssignedIdentities.Keys -contains $identityId
} | Format-Table Name, ResourceGroupName

# List role assignments for this identity
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'RoleManagement.Read.All'
$principalId = (Get-AzUserAssignedIdentity -Name $identityName -ResourceGroupName 'MyResourceGroup').PrincipalId
Get-AzRoleAssignment -ObjectId $principalId | Format-Table RoleDefinitionName, Scope
Common Errors and Troubleshooting
Symptom
Cause
Fix
ManagedIdentityCredential: multiple identities available
Resource has multiple user-assigned identities and client_id not specified
Set AZURE_CLIENT_ID env var or pass client_id= to ManagedIdentityCredential
403 on Key Vault after attaching identity
RBAC role propagation delay (up to 2 minutes)
Wait 1-2 minutes after role assignment before testing; RBAC changes are not instant
Identity detached after VM reimaging
OS reimaging resets the VM's identity configuration
Re-attach via az vm identity assign after reimaging, or use a VM scale set with identity in the model
Federated credential not working in AKS
Service account not annotated with azure.workload.identity/client-id
Add annotation: kubectl annotate sa my-service-account azure.workload.identity/client-id=<client-id>
az identity delete fails with 'resource in use'
Identity is still attached to one or more resources
Detach from all resources first; then delete
Summary
User-assigned managed identities are standalone Azure resources that decouple the identity lifecycle from any individual compute resource, enabling sharing across a fleet.
Create as a standalone resource (az identity create); get the clientId and principalId from the output.
Assign RBAC roles once to the principalId — all attached resources inherit those permissions.
Attach to VMs, App Services, Functions, Container Instances, and more using identity.type = UserAssigned.
When multiple identities are attached, set AZURE_CLIENT_ID in app settings to select the correct one in DefaultAzureCredential.
The identity survives resource deletion — stable principal ID means no role re-assignments when recreating compute.
Ideal for autoscaling fleets (VMSS), pre-provisioned identities, and any scenario where multiple services share the same access requirements.
Both system-assigned and user-assigned can coexist on one resource using identity.type = SystemAssigned, UserAssigned.
